description: IND-enabling requirements, manufacturing constraints, and regulatory considerations for genetic medicines.

facts.context: IND-enabling toxicology studies for gene therapy typically require:
facts.truth: GLP studies with clinical vector plus biodistribution
distractors: in vitro cytotoxicity screens with surrogate vectors
retrospective analysis of prior clinical trial data
computational toxicity modeling without animal studies

facts.context: Species selection for gene therapy tox studies considers:
facts.truth: vector tropism, transgene expression, immune relevance
distractors: breeding cycle length and colony maintenance cost
genome size similarity to human reference sequence
body mass scaling for allometric dose conversion

facts.context: Biodistribution studies for gene therapy must assess:
facts.truth: vector DNA/RNA persistence and germline distribution
distractors: transgene mRNA expression in target tissue alone
serum protein pharmacokinetics over 24-hour window
capsid antibody titers measured at single timepoint

facts.truth: vector excretion in body fluids for transmission risk
distractors: internal tissue biodistribution at injection site region
viral genome replication rate within transduced cells
capsid protein degradation kinetics in cell culture

facts.context: Potency assays for gene therapy products must:
facts.truth: measure biological activity tied to mechanism of action
distractors: quantify total viral particle concentration by qPCR
assess vector genome packaging by electron microscopy
determine capsid protein ratio using mass spectrometry

facts.context: Identity testing for AAV gene therapy includes:
facts.truth: capsid serotype, transgene sequence, genome integrity
distractors: total protein concentration measured by Bradford assay
endotoxin quantification using limulus amebocyte lysate
osmolality and pH measurement of formulation buffer

facts.context: Purity specifications for AAV vectors address:
facts.truth: empty capsid ratio, residual HCP/DNA, endotoxin
distractors: transgene coding sequence fidelity and codon usage
capsid thermal stability measured by differential scanning
vector infectivity titer determined by transduction assay

facts.context: Manufacturing process changes require comparability studies demonstrating:
facts.truth: equivalent quality, potency, and safety across changes
distractors: reduced production cost per dose after optimization
increased manufacturing throughput at commercial scale
faster turnaround time for batch release testing

facts.context: Long-term follow-up (LTFU) for gene therapy patients is required because:
facts.truth: delayed adverse events require 5-15 year monitoring
distractors: acute infusion reactions resolve within 30 days post-dose
vector genome integration patterns stabilize after one month
immune memory responses peak within initial treatment period

facts.context: Pediatric gene therapy trials have additional considerations including:
facts.truth: growth effects, developing immunity, longer risk window
distractors: weight-based dosing adjustments using standard allometry
parental consent requirements for enrollment eligibility
formulation palatability testing for oral administration

facts.context: Accelerated approval for gene therapies may be granted based on:
distractors: preclinical efficacy data from relevant animal models
Phase 1 dose-escalation safety and tolerability results
manufacturing readiness assessment at commercial scale

facts.context: Gene therapy pricing and reimbursement challenges include:
facts.truth: high upfront cost for curative one-time treatment
distractors: standard pharmaceutical tiered pricing models apply
annual subscription reimbursement from commercial payers
volume-based discount structures for hospital purchasing
